content:
Stories (continued) — HRREC-82977

StoryGap score (%)PM lensQA lensXO lensVerdictSuggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)

HRREC-92030
PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of valid email addresses

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of valid email addresses: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Agency / UDMF: duplicate agency submissions depend on BP CRF vs UDMF-aware rule; isolation tests need explicit internal vs agency accounts.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92030 (PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of va…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of valid email addresses: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92030 (PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of va…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92030: PH: Recruiter can select To address from list of valid email addresses — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched Recruiting Email PRU/PU paths (Candidate Email create, External/Internal candidate parms) for send/receive wiring.
  • XO MCP — risk: attachment handling spans multiple services—confirm which layer enforces limits for this flow.
  • Finding: HRREC-92030 — Accessibility and empty states are easy to regress—pair visual checks with keyboard-only navigation notes.
  • Recommended next step: Run one cross-browser smoke on the primary path after each merge train batch for this epic.

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-92030 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Recruiter can select To address from list of valid email addresses
Then UI, persisted thread state, and captured network payloads align for this slice

HRREC-92035
PH: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream Timeline and Candidate Communications

50% — PM milestone wording vs QA export parity vs XO event cardinality (thin inventory in Jira).

  • Jira — PH: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream Timeline and Candidate Communications: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Purge: Recruiting Communications PDT removes conversation messages, participants, recruiting email attachments, notification events—align recruiter-visible UI after purge with privacy comms.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92035 (PH: Email communications are visible in Activity St…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream Timeline and …: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92035 (PH: Email communications are visible in Activity St…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92035: PH: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream Timeline and Candidate Communic… — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation Message (+ Derived, Status, Sender) for delivery tags, read/unread, purge visibility.
  • XO MCP — risk: attachment handling spans multiple services—confirm which layer enforces limits for this flow.
  • Finding: HRREC-92035 — Accessibility and empty states are easy to regress—pair visual checks with keyboard-only navigation notes.
  • Recommended next step: Attach the exact mail mode and template name used when the behaviour reproduces (or fails).

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-92035 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream Timeline and Candidate Communications
Then UI, persisted thread state, and captured network payloads align for this slice

HRREC-92036
PH: Send Message (With Attachments)

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Send Message (With Attachments): confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Agency / UDMF: duplicate agency submissions depend on BP CRF vs UDMF-aware rule; isolation tests need explicit internal vs agency accounts.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92036 (PH: Send Message (With Attachments)): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Send Message (With Attachments): record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Attachment and size limits differ by channel—log count, MiB, and virus-scan rejection separately in defects.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92036 (PH: Send Message (With Attachments)): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92036: PH: Send Message (With Attachments) — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched Recruiting Email PRU/PU paths (Candidate Email create, External/Internal candidate parms) for send/receive wiring.
  • XO MCP — risk: vendor mail rejections may surface as generic errors—pair UI copy with vendor status rows.
  • Finding: HRREC-92036 — Edge cases (slow network, double submit) are implied more than written—false-positive bug risk until spelled out.
  • Recommended next step: Attach the exact mail mode and template name used when the behaviour reproduces (or fails).

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-92036 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Send Message (With Attachments)
Then UI, persisted thread state, and captured network payloads align for this slice
